name: SceneView
description: The #1 open-source 3D & AR SDK for Android — Compose-native scenes powered by Google Filament and ARCore.

name: SceneView — 3D & AR SDK for Android
description: The modern, Compose-native 3D and AR SDK for Android. Build immersive 3D scenes and augmented reality experiences with Jetpack Compose, powered by Google Filament and ARCore.

name: SceneView
description: The #1 Android 3D & AR SDK — Jetpack Compose composables powered by Google Filament and ARCore. Drop-in Scene{} and ARScene{} for model viewing, AR placement, physics, dynamic environments, and immersive experiences.

acceptedAnswer.text: SceneView is the #1 open-source 3D and AR SDK for Android. It provides Compose-native declarative APIs (Scene{} and ARScene{}) to build immersive 3D and augmented reality experiences, powered by Google Filament for rendering and ARCore for AR tracking. It is the official successor to the deprecated Google Sceneform.

mainEntity.name: How do I add 3D content to my Android app?

acceptedAnswer.text: Add the SceneView dependency (io.github.sceneview:sceneview:3.2.0), then use the Scene{} composable with ModelNode to load and display glTF/GLB 3D models. It takes just 5 lines of Kotlin code to display a 3D model in your Compose UI.
Yes, SceneView is completely free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license. You can use it in personal and commercial projects without any licensing fees.

SceneView is the official community successor to Google Sceneform, which was deprecated in 2021. SceneView provides a modern, Compose-native API that is actively maintained and significantly more capable than the original Sceneform.

Yes, SceneView provides full ARCore integration through its arsceneview module. Use the ARScene{} composable with AnchorNode, AugmentedImageNode, AugmentedFaceNode, and more to build AR experiences with plane detection, image tracking, face mesh, cloud anchors, and geospatial features.

SceneView currently supports Android (API 24+, all architectures). The v4.0 roadmap includes Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP) expansion to iOS (via Filament Metal), desktop, and web. SceneView also provides an MCP server for AI assistant integration.
